docs.title: Welcome to WotLK Extensions
docs.text:
WotLK Extensions is a native DLL extension framework for World of Warcraft 3.3.5a (Wrath of the Lich King) focused on expanding the capabilities of the original client through modern runtime enhancements.

The project provides a foundation for extending the 3.3.5a game client beyond its original limitations, enabling developers to implement custom systems, graphical enhancements, runtime hooks, client-side features, and experimental functionality without modifying the core game executable directly.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
World of Warcraft 3.3.5a remains one of the most widely used versions for emulation and custom server development, but its client architecture has many hardcoded limitations. WotLK Extensions exists to provide a flexible and modular way to extend that client through native code injection and runtime systems.

This framework is intended for developers who want to:

  • Extend client functionality through DLL-based systems
  • Hook and modify client behavior at runtime
  • Build custom graphical or UI enhancements
  • Add new client-side systems and experimental features
  • Prototype advanced modifications for private server development
  • Research and explore the WoW 3.3.5a client architecture

docs.title: Project Goals
docs.text:
WotLK Extensions is designed with several core goals in mind:

  • Modularity – Build reusable extension systems without tightly coupling changes to the client
  • Runtime Flexibility – Enable feature development through hooks, injected systems, and dynamic behavior
  • Developer-Focused Tooling – Provide a practical foundation for experimentation and advanced client engineering
  • Maintainability – Keep extension logic organized, clean, and scalable
  • Modern Development Practices – Apply structured native development techniques to legacy game client modding

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
WotLK Extensions is not a standalone game client or emulator. Instead, it acts as an extension layer for the WoW 3.3.5a client, allowing developers to implement functionality that would otherwise require direct executable patching or invasive client modification.

Potential use cases include:

  • Client-side rendering extensions
  • Native hook systems
  • Custom UI integrations
  • Debugging and developer tooling
  • Experimental game systems
  • Runtime behavior modification
  • Research and reverse engineering projects

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
This documentation covers the architecture, systems, implementation details, and developer workflows used throughout the project.

Whether you are exploring the codebase, integrating your own modules, or building advanced client-side systems, these docs are intended to provide a clear reference point for working with WotLK Extensions.

docs.title: Setup & Installation
docs.text: This guide walks through the complete setup process for WotLK Extensions, from cloning the repository to patching your WoW 3.3.5a client and launching the extension framework.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before building WotLK Extensions, make sure you have the following installed:

  • Git
  • CMake (latest stable version recommended)
  • Visual Studio 2022 (with C++ desktop development tools / latest Visual Studio version may also work)
  • A clean World of Warcraft 3.3.5a client

Clean Client Required

The patcher must be used on an unmodified WoW.exe. If your client has already been patched, modified, or altered by another tool, restore a clean executable before continuing.

docs.title: 1. Clone the Repository
docs.text:
Clone the repository using Git:

Cloning the Repository
git clone https://github.com/Alyst3r/WotLK-Extensions.git
cd WotLK-Extensions

docs.title: 2. Configure the Project with CMake
docs.text: WotLK Extensions uses CMake to configure and generate the Visual Studio solution.

docs.title: Create a Build Directory
docs.text:
It is recommended to generate build files in a separate directory:

Create Build Directory
mkdir build
cd build

docs.title: Configure the Project
docs.text:
Open CMake GUI (or use the CLI) and configure the project with:

  • Source Directory → your WotLK-Extensions repository
  • Build Directory → your build folder

During configuration, CMake will expose the available patch options.

These options control which features are compiled into Patcher.exe, allowing you to decide which client modifications will be applied when patching WoW.exe.

Patch Options

Image below shows an example of the CMake Source and Build directory configuration, the paths should match your local setup.

docs.title: Configure Patch Options
docs.text:
After setting the source and build directories, click Configure, if anything lights up red, please click Configure again. CMake will display a list of configurable options, including the available patches.

Review the available patch options and enable the features you want included in the patcher.

Patch Selection

Only enable the patches you intend to use. These options are compiled into Patcher.exe during the build process.

Below is an example of the patch options you may see in CMake:

docs.title: 3. Generate the Visual Studio Project
docs.text:
Once configuration is complete:

  1. Click Generate
  2. Let CMake create the Visual Studio solution/project files inside your build directory
  3. After generation, if everything was successful, you should now be able to click Open Project in CMake to launch Visual Studio directly.

This will generate a standalone Visual Studio solution, typically inside, but it depends on your build directory configuration:

WotLK-Extensions/build/

docs.title: 4. Open the Project in Visual Studio
docs.text:
Open the Project

If you clicked Open Project in CMake after generation, Visual Studio should open automatically. You can skip this step.

After generation:

  • Navigate to your build folder
  • Open the generated Visual Studio solution (.sln)

Example:

WotLK-Extensions/build/WotLKExtensions.sln

docs.title: 5. Build the Project
docs.text:
Inside Visual Studio:

  1. Select your build configuration:
    • Debug
    • RelWithDebInfo (recommended for development)
  2. Build the solution

Visual Studio will compile the project and output the required binaries.

docs.title: 6. Locate the Generated Files
docs.text:
After a successful build, the compiled files will be located inside:

build/bin/<Debug|RelWithDebInfo>/

You should see:

  • Patcher.exe
  • WotLKExtensions.dll

docs.title: 7. Copy Files to Your WoW Client Folder
docs.text:
This step is critical.

Copy the following files:

  • Patcher.exe
  • WotLKExtensions.dll

Into your World of Warcraft 3.3.5a client folder, where WoW.exe is located.

Example:

World of Warcraft/
├── WoW.exe
├── Patcher.exe
├── WotLKExtensions.dll

Important

Patcher.exe and WotLKExtensions.dll must be placed inside the WoW client directory, next to WoW.exe.

Do not run the patcher from another folder. Do not leave the DLL in your build output directory. The files must live directly inside the client folder.

docs.title: 8. Patch the WoW Client
docs.text:
Inside your WoW 3.3.5a client folder:

  1. Drag WoW.exe onto Patcher.exe
  2. Let it apply the selected patches to WoW.exe

Do Not Patch an Already Patched Client

Patcher.exe expects a clean, original WoW.exe.

Running the patcher on an already patched executable may fail or produce unexpected behavior.

Patching Process

If patching is successful, it should simply blink, if any errors occur, the patcher will display an error message. The patcher will modify WoW.exe in-place, applying the selected patches. After patching, WoW.exe will be modified to automatically load WotLKExtensions.dll on startup.

docs.title: 9. Launch the Game
docs.text:
After patching is complete:

  • Launch WoW.exe normally

If patching was successful:

  • WoW.exe will automatically load and inject WotLKExtensions.dll
  • The extension framework will initialize during startup

No manual DLL injector is required.

docs.title: Final Folder Layout
docs.text:
Your client folder should look similar to this:

World of Warcraft/
├── WoW.exe
├── Patcher.exe
├── WotLKExtensions.dll
├── Data/
├── Interface/
└── ...

docs.title: Patcher does not work
docs.text:
Check:

  • Is WoW.exe clean and unmodified?
  • Is Patcher.exe in the same folder as WoW.exe?
  • Is WotLKExtensions.dll in the same folder as WoW.exe?

docs.title: DLL does not load
docs.text:
Check:

  • Was patching successful?
  • Is WotLKExtensions.dll still present in the WoW client folder?
  • Are you launching the patched WoW.exe?

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
Setup follows this workflow:

Clone Repository
    ↓
Configure with CMake
    ↓
Enable Desired Patch Options
    ↓
Generate Visual Studio Project
    ↓
Build Solution
    ↓
Copy Patcher.exe + WotLKExtensions.dll to WoW Client Folder
    ↓
Run Patcher.exe
    ↓
Launch WoW.exe
    ↓
WotLK Extensions loads automatically
